mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and rising recession probability, which reduces expected corporate earnings and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation and declining retail sales indicate demand destruction and margin compression, forcing equity markets to price in lower future earnings over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint regression coefficient for the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not decline when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rebounding above its 3-month moving average signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial production rebounds are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals weakening consumer demand and construction sector contraction, which reduces near-term economic growth expectations and equity valuations.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of triggered instances, or the housing starts threshold shows no predictive power after adjusting for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation exceeding 8 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 2 percent creates a deeply negative real rate environment that forces aggressive future tightening, depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between inflation and rates shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or equity markets rally despite the negative real rate divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 300 thousand monthly sustains consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, supporting positive quarterly equity returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during strong payroll months are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the signal reverses direction when controlling for wage inflation dynamics.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates economic stagnation and reduced corporate profit growth, triggering negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of low-GDP-growth instances, or the GDP growth threshold loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
